items.headline: Warriors' Moses Moody Suffers Apparent Serious Knee Injury in OT Win
items.summary: Golden State Warriors guard Moses Moody was carted off the court late in overtime against the Dallas Mavericks after suffering what appeared to be a serious left knee injury. Moody had scored 23 points before the incident.

items.headline: Stephen Curry Expected to Return for Play-In Tournament Despite Ailing Knee
items.summary: Despite an ailing right knee, Warriors superstar Stephen Curry is expected to be available for the play-in tournament, according to coach Steve Kerr.

items.headline: Siakam's 37 Points End Pacers' Record Losing Streak
items.summary: Pascal Siakam erupted for 37 points and delivered a crucial game-winning block in the final seconds, leading the Indiana Pacers to a 128-126 victory over the Orlando Magic and snapping their franchise-record 16-game losing streak.

items.headline: Warriors Outlast Mavericks in Overtime Thriller
items.summary: Golden State extended Dallas' home losing streak to 12 games.

items.headline: Bulls Top Rockets in High-Scoring Affair
Pacers Snap Skid with Narrow Win Over Magic
Spurs Dominate Heat on the Road
Thunder Roll Past Wizards
Cavaliers Edge Pelicans
Pascal Siakam Leads Pacers to Victory
items.summary: Siakam dropped 37 points and made a game-sealing block against the Magic.

items.headline: Victor Wembanyama Dominates for Spurs
items.summary: Wembanyama recorded 26 points in a dominant performance against the Heat, solidifying his MVP-caliber season.

items.headline: Moses Moody's Strong Outing Cut Short
items.summary: Before his unfortunate injury, Moody contributed 23 points for the Warriors in their overtime win.

items.headline: Deni Avdija Continues Stellar Season
items.summary: Israeli forward Deni Avdija is having a career-best season for the Blazers, averaging 24.2 points, 7.0 rebounds, and 6.8 assists per game. He recently posted 23 points and 14 assists against Denver on March 22nd.

items.headline: Rookie Amen Thompson Impressing for Rockets
items.summary: Houston's Amen Thompson has been a versatile force, averaging a combined 35.7 points, rebounds, and assists over his last 10 games, including grabbing at least 11 rebounds in four of his last five contests.

items.headline: Bulls Rookie Matas Buzelis Making Impact
items.summary: Matas Buzelis has been a key contributor for Chicago, averaging a combined 28.2 points and rebounds over his last 10 games, scoring 20+ points in six of those contests.

items.headline: Giannis Antetokounmpo Out with Knee Injury
items.summary: Milwaukee Bucks star Giannis Antetokounmpo is currently sidelined with a left knee hyperextension and bone bruise.

items.headline: Star Players Battling Injuries Across the League
items.summary: Several notable players are dealing with injuries: Kawhi Leonard (LAC) is questionable with an ankle sprain, Bradley Beal (LAC) is out for the season with a hip fracture, Franz Wagner (ORL) is out with an ankle injury, and Kings' stars Domantas Sabonis and Zach LaVine are both out for the remainder of the season.

items.headline: Eastern Conference Playoff Picture Tightens
items.summary: As of March 23rd, the top teams in the Eastern Conference include the Detroit Pistons (51-19), Boston Celtics (47-24), New York Knicks (47-25), and Cleveland Cavaliers (44-27).

items.headline: Western Conference Race Heats Up
items.summary: The Western Conference sees the Oklahoma City Thunder (56-15) and San Antonio Spurs (53-18) leading the pack, followed by the Los Angeles Lakers (46-25), Houston Rockets (43-27), and Denver Nuggets (44-28).

items.headline: Mavericks' Home Losing Streak Extends to 12 Games
items.summary: The Dallas Mavericks' struggles continued last night, extending their franchise-worst home losing streak to 12 games after their overtime loss to the Warriors.

items.headline: Teams Eyeing 2026 NBA Draft Prospects
items.summary: With three blue-chip prospects expected in the 2026 NBA Draft (Darryn Peterson, AJ Dybantsa, Cameron Boozer), several teams like the Pacers, Wizards, Nets, Jazz, and Kings are reportedly prioritizing their draft position.
